methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Observable Space, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Dan Roelker and Rick Hedrick, has raised $20M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including ClearSpace, Planet Labs, and Rocket Lab.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $20M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for Series A advancement, implying a $40M valuation. Peer-median triangulation references four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: ClearSpace at $400M (Series A), Hubble Network at $400M (Series B), Sift at $274M (Series B), and Aalyria at $1.3B (Series B). This peer set yields a $400M median with a range of $274M–$1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied—the Space & Aerospace public sector median trades at 49.8x price-to-sales across six tickers, but Observable Space's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated valuation range is $34M–$460M, with a median of $220M derived from the two applicable methods. Key uncertainties include the early-stage revenue profile relative to later-stage peers (which compress the median estimate), stage distribution within the comparable set, and the absence of Observable Space's current financial metrics needed for P/S or revenue-multiple anchoring.
